$description.$value: Mode-independent raw values. Never referenced by components directly.

sans.$description: Primary sans — Inter with system fallbacks

display.$description: Display variant — optical size optimised for large text (falls back to Inter)

$description.$value: Purpose-driven tokens with mode-keyed values for light/dark. Consumed by components.

raised.$description: Cards and raised panels — slightly lighter than page bg in dark mode for elevation

overlay.$description: Modals and popovers — highest elevation surface

onAccent.$description: Text on accent-coloured fills — stays white in both modes

subtle.$description: Tinted backgrounds — flip from light tint to dark tint

$description.$value: Mode-independent — spacing is the same in light and dark
Typographic roles. Each role is a composite style grouping family+size+weight+lineHeight+letterSpacing.
Complete text styles — ready to apply as a full typographic spec
Hero display text — 72px
Small uppercase label — section eyebrows, category tags
Shadows need heavier opacity in dark mode to remain visible
Elevation tiers — each combines surface color, shadow, and border for consistent depth.
No elevation — inline with background
Slight lift — secondary cards, sidebar items
Standard card elevation — primary content surfaces
Floating UI — popovers, dropdowns, tooltips
Top-layer — modals, full-screen sheets
Density modes — scale padding and control heights. Swap values to change global density.
Standard density — balanced for desktop and mobile
Dense UI — data tables, admin dashboards
Relaxed spacing — reading interfaces, marketing
Component tokens reference semantic tokens — dark mode propagates automatically.
Text input — sm/md/lg sizes
Card container — consumes elevation tokens, supports sm/md/lg sizes
Badge — sm/md sizes, semantic variants
Tooltip — uses elevation.overlay, inverted colors
Modal dialog — uses elevation.modal, sm/md/lg/xl sizes
Checkbox input — used in forms and selection lists
Radio button input
Toggle switch — on/off binary control
Select dropdown — uses input tokens as base
Data table — rows, cells, headers
Vertical item list
Visual separator line
Tabbed navigation — indicator and active states
Hierarchical navigation trail
Numbered page navigation
Inline message boxes for info, success, warning, danger
Floating notification — transient feedback
Linear progress indicator
Loading placeholder for content
User avatar — image, initials, or icon
